name: Flat-Pack Compliance Test
description: Drag and rotate unfamiliar parts into a physically connected assembly, then survive a load test that exposes every fake joint.
natural_language: Assemble the device correctly. It must remain intact during the compliance test. Drag and rotate every asymmetric rigid part, lock only geometrically matching keyed sockets, then survive gravity and the complete oscillating load sequence.

metadata.variant_count_kind: part poses, topology mirroring, keyed orientation, load waveform, palette, and initial rack permutation
metadata.notes: Matter.js runs symmetric body collisions and actual constraints; the server independently replays keyed socket poses, graph topology, collision clearance, and a deterministic compliance sensor contract.
